title: rubus ambigens
intro: Rubus ambigens, also known as the thimbleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. It is native to North America and is characterized by its edible fruit, which resembles a raspberry but has a hollow center. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Rubus ambigens can be prepared as a herbal tea, juice, or tincture, and is also used in jams and preserves, making it a versatile and nutritious addition to a healthy diet.
